target.title: fix: scrub serialized tool-call text from replies
target.body:
## Summary
- scrub serialized `[tool:*]` and `<function=...>` tool-call parameter blocks from user-facing reply text before Discord final delivery
- consume adjacent XMLish `<parameter=...>` blocks plus an optional `</function>` close while preserving visible reply text
- cover the shared SDK scrubber and Discord final-send boundary with regression tests

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Discord final replies can include serialized tool-call markup with XMLish `<parameter=...>` payload blocks. After this patch, the shared front-channel sanitizer removes the internal tool-call block and keeps the visible reply text.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout at PR head `8c77c461290ae4e3a3d9f4fe8b3dec0c6f10eaf2`, Node `v24.14.0`, using the real `extensions/discord/src/monitor/reply-safety.ts` sanitizer path through `node --import tsx`. This proof command did not use the Vitest test harness or mocked Discord delivery.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import { sanitizeDiscordFrontChannelReplyPayloads } from './extensions/discord/src/monitor/reply-safety.ts';

const raw = [
  'Intro text.',
  '[tool:exec]',
  '<parameter=command>',
  'echo hidden-internal-command',
  '</parameter>',
  '',
  '<function=exec>',
  '<parameter=command>',
  'printf hidden-second-command',
  '</parameter>',
  '<parameter=timeout_ms>',
  '1000',
  '</parameter>',
  '</function>',
  '',
  'Visible reply.',
].join('\n');

const payloads = sanitizeDiscordFrontChannelReplyPayloads([{ text: raw }], { kind: 'final' });
console.log(JSON.stringify(payloads, null, 2));
EOF
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
[
  {
    "text": "Intro text.\n\nVisible reply."
  }
]
```

Observed result after fix: the sanitizer returned one final Discord payload containing only `Intro text.` and `Visible reply.`. The serialized `[tool:exec]`, `<function=exec>`, both `<parameter=...>` blocks, and their payload values were absent from the delivered text payload.

What was not tested: live Discord/Mantis delivery was not run from this environment because no Discord/Mantis credentials were present. The local proof exercised the real Discord front-channel sanitizer path; the focused Discord boundary test covers the final-send handoff.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/tool-payload.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/reply-delivery.test.ts` (26 tests passed)
- `node --import tsx --input-type=module ...` against `sanitizeDiscordFrontChannelReplyPayloads` produced the JSON output shown in Real behavior proof
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
